main.description: A single iteration of a project — one cycle of the community's async-sync rhythm (e.g. one EPTSS round, one book club month, one art-prompt week). Phases describe the round's schedule of named events; communities define their own phase vocabularies.

project.description: AT-URI of the site.eptss.project record this round belongs to.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier scoped to the project (e.g. round number, theme name, or month).

subject.description: AT-URI of the site.eptss.subject record chosen for this round. Absent until selection completes.

phases.description: Ordered list of scheduled events that make up the round's lifecycle. Each community defines its own phase vocabulary (e.g. EPTSS uses 'proposalsOpen', 'votingOpens', 'creationBegins', 'submissionsDue', 'gatheringAt'). Generic clients render this as a timeline; specialized clients look up known phase names.

links.description: Optional external links associated with the round — e.g. a listening-party playlist, a discussion thread, a gallery view, a release notes page.

description.description: Optional human-readable note about this phase (e.g. 'Holiday extension — extra week given for covers').
